key_insight: ePBS changes block building from a relay-mediated auction to a strategic two-stage game, creating a ratchet effect where early bid disclosure can distort later bidding and block allocation.
engineering_relevance: The paper suggests protocol design choices in Ethereum can materially affect revenue, allocation efficiency, and proposer-builder incentives, with a TEE-based sidecar offering a practical path toward more commitment and less manipulation.
method: Models PBS and ePBS as restrictions of the same imperfect-information two-stage auction with verifiable messages.
Derives perfect Bayesian equilibria in tractable cases and uses calibrated no-regret learning for finite games.
Formulates a TEE sidecar mechanism as a bilinear optimization problem to approximate commitment benefits.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports exact equilibrium results, calibrated finite-game learning, and conservative benchmarks where the proposed TEE design raises proposer revenue by about 25% over the first-price benchmark.
watch_item: The TEE approach depends on trusted hardware and a feasible deployment model, so security, adoption, and implementation overhead may limit real-world effectiveness.
paper_title: From PBS to ePBS: the Microstructure of Block Building
